name: pending-stub
description: Placeholder pending increment. Will be properly planned when increment 40 completes.
sprintGoal: Advance kind-cluster HA validation chain: create kind-smoke.sh scaffold (HA-005a), verify chaos-mesh runtime (TEST-004b) and wiremock runtime (TEST-005b), wire check-limits.py into ha-gate.sh (HA-011), close code-server extension autarky gap (DEVEX-015), add SMART CRD-spec citation guidance (CEREMONY-012), and clear 6 accumulated review-confirmation stories.

stories.title: Create scripts/test/kind-smoke.sh: static-verifiable scaffold covering PLATFORM-001 through PLATFORM-004
stories.description: Create scripts/test/kind-smoke.sh that encodes all PLATFORM-001 through PLATFORM-004 acceptance criteria as executable test functions (cert-manager, sealed-secrets, Harbor, Keycloak). The script must define one test function per PLATFORM story, print a PASS/FAIL summary table, and pass shellcheck -S error with no warnings. The exact commands for each test come from the acceptance criteria in prd/increment-21-platform-foundations.json.
acceptanceCriteria: shellcheck -S error scripts/test/kind-smoke.sh exits 0
grep -c 'helm\|kubectl' scripts/test/kind-smoke.sh returns a value >= 4 (at least one invocation per PLATFORM-00x test)
grep -E 'PASS|FAIL' scripts/test/kind-smoke.sh exits 0 (PASS and FAIL literals appear in summary output logic)
stories.testPlan: shellcheck -S error scripts/test/kind-smoke.sh; grep -c 'helm\|kubectl' scripts/test/kind-smoke.sh; grep -E 'PASS|FAIL' scripts/test/kind-smoke.sh; show all output verbatim.

smart.notes: Static-verifiable split of HA-005 (Strategy D). Exact deliverable (scripts/test/kind-smoke.sh) named. All 3 ACs are shell commands with binary pass/fail. No running cluster required. Scope: 1 file created.

stories.title: Deploy platform/charts/chaos-mesh/ to kind-sovereign-test and run a pod failure experiment
stories.description: Install platform/charts/chaos-mesh/ into kind-sovereign-test in namespace chaos-testing. Create kind/fixtures/podchaos-test.yaml PodChaos experiment fixture targeting a test pod. Apply the fixture and verify the experiment recovers (AllRecovered=True) within 120 seconds. This validates runtime behavior beyond static lint.
acceptanceCriteria: helm install chaos-mesh platform/charts/chaos-mesh/ --kube-context kind-sovereign-test --namespace chaos-testing --create-namespace --wait --timeout 3m exits 0
kubectl get pods -n chaos-testing --context kind-sovereign-test shows all pods Running

kubectl apply -f kind/fixtures/podchaos-test.yaml --context kind-sovereign-test exits 0 and within 120 seconds kubectl describe podchaos podchaos-test -n chaos-testing --context kind-sovereign-test shows AllRecovered=True (chaos-mesh v2.6.3 terminal recovery state is 'Not Injected', not 'Finished')
kind/fixtures/podchaos-test.yaml exists and is valid YAML
stories.testPlan: helm install chaos-mesh platform/charts/chaos-mesh/ --kube-context kind-sovereign-test --namespace chaos-testing --create-namespace --wait --timeout 3m && kubectl get pods -n chaos-testing --context kind-sovereign-test && kubectl apply -f kind/fixtures/podchaos-test.yaml --context kind-sovereign-test && sleep 30 && kubectl describe podchaos podchaos-test -n chaos-testing --context kind-sovereign-test | grep -E 'AllRecovered|Phase'; show all output verbatim.

smart.notes: Runtime-verifiable split of TEST-004 (Strategy D). AC3 corrected: 'phase=Finished' replaced with 'AllRecovered=True' per empirical verification in sprint 37 (chaos-mesh v2.6.3 uses Not Injected as terminal state). Measurable score raised to 5. Achievable score 4: requires kind-sovereign-test (KIND-001a, KIND-001b both accepted in sprints 31 and 32). TimeBound score 4: 120s window is empirical but reasonable.

stories.returnedReason: AC3 asserted 'phase=Finished' but chaos-mesh v2.6.3 terminal recovery state is 'Not Injected' (AllRecovered=True); implementation complete, AC corrected for next sprint

stories.title: Deploy platform/charts/wiremock/ to kind-sovereign-test and verify admin REST API accepts stub mappings
stories.description: Install platform/charts/wiremock/ into kind-sovereign-test in namespace wiremock-test. Port-forward the Wiremock service and verify the admin API accepts a stub mapping POST to /__admin/mappings (expect HTTP 201) and that a matching GET to the mapped URL returns the configured response body. This validates runtime behavior beyond static lint.
acceptanceCriteria: helm install wiremock platform/charts/wiremock/ --kube-context kind-sovereign-test --namespace wiremock-test --create-namespace --wait --timeout 3m exits 0
kubectl get pods -n wiremock-test --context kind-sovereign-test shows all pods Running

kubectl port-forward -n wiremock-test svc/wiremock 8080:8080 --context kind-sovereign-test & sleep 2 && curl -s -o /dev/null -w '%{http_code}' -X POST http://localhost:8080/__admin/mappings -H 'Content-Type: application/json' -d '{"request":{"method":"GET","url":"/api/test"},"response":{"status":200,"body":"ok"}}' returns 201
curl -s http://localhost:8080/api/test returns 'ok'
stories.testPlan: helm install wiremock platform/charts/wiremock/ --kube-context kind-sovereign-test --namespace wiremock-test --create-namespace --wait --timeout 3m && kubectl get pods -n wiremock-test --context kind-sovereign-test && kubectl port-forward -n wiremock-test svc/wiremock 8080:8080 --context kind-sovereign-test & sleep 2 && curl -X POST http://localhost:8080/__admin/mappings -H 'Content-Type: application/json' -d '...' && curl http://localhost:8080/api/test; show all output verbatim.

smart.notes: Runtime-verifiable split of TEST-005 (Strategy D). Correct path platform/charts/wiremock/ specified. AC3 (curl returns 201) and AC4 (curl returns 'ok') verify actual runtime admin API behavior. Achievable score 4: requires kind-sovereign-test (KIND-001a, KIND-001b both accepted). TimeBound score 5: all ACs have deterministic expected outputs.

stories.title: Create scripts/check-limits.py — validates every container has resource requests and limits; wire into ha-gate.sh and validate.yml
stories.description: The agent.md quality gate pattern 'helm template platform/charts/<name>/ | python3 scripts/check-limits.py' is documented as mandatory but scripts/check-limits.py does not exist. Create scripts/check-limits.py following the validate.yml pattern: reads YAML from stdin (helm template output), walks every containers and initContainers spec, and exits 1 with a list of failing containers if any are missing resources.requests or resources.limits. Integrate into scripts/ha-gate.sh by adding a check-limits.py invocation for each chart. Add a CI step to .github/workflows/validate.yml (or ha-gate.yml) that pipes each chart's rendered template through check-limits.py on every PR touching platform/charts/.
acceptanceCriteria: scripts/check-limits.py exists — ls scripts/check-limits.py exits 0
python3 scripts/check-limits.py exits 0 when piped a Deployment with complete resource requests and limits on all containers

python3 scripts/check-limits.py exits 1 when piped a Deployment with a container missing resources.limits — python3 -c "import subprocess,sys; r=subprocess.run(['python3','scripts/check-limits.py'],input=b'apiVersion: apps/v1
kind: Deployment
spec:
  template:
    spec:
      containers:
      - name: test
        image: test:1.0',capture_output=True); sys.exit(0 if r.returncode == 1 else 1)" exits 0
grep 'check-limits' scripts/ha-gate.sh exits 0 — the script is invoked in ha-gate.sh for each chart
shellcheck -S error scripts/ha-gate.sh exits 0
stories.testPlan: ls scripts/check-limits.py && python3 scripts/check-limits.py --help && grep 'check-limits' scripts/ha-gate.sh && shellcheck -S error scripts/ha-gate.sh && bash scripts/ha-gate.sh --dry-run

stories.title: code-server pre-installs VS Code extensions at startup from Harbor — no external marketplace calls
stories.description: code-server downloads VS Code extensions from marketplace.visualstudio.com by default, violating T1 Sovereignty. Add an extensionRegistry field to platform/charts/code-server/values.yaml that defaults to '{{ .Values.global.imageRegistry }}'. Add an extensions: [] array to values.yaml with a default set of 3 extensions: redhat.vscode-yaml, ms-kubernetes-tools.vscode-kubernetes-tools, timonwong.shellcheck-snappy. Add a lifecycle.postStart or initContainer script to the code-server Deployment that runs code-server --install-extension for each entry in .Values.extensions, using vsix files served from the extensionRegistry. The script must be idempotent (skip already-installed extensions). No template in platform/charts/code-server/templates/ may reference marketplace.visualstudio.com or open-vsx.org.
acceptanceCriteria: platform/charts/code-server/values.yaml contains an extensions: field with at least 3 default entries — grep -A5 'extensions:' platform/charts/code-server/values.yaml shows 3+ lines
platform/charts/code-server/values.yaml contains an extensionRegistry field — grep 'extensionRegistry' platform/charts/code-server/values.yaml exits 0
helm template platform/charts/code-server/ | grep 'install-extension' exits 0 — the extension install command is rendered in the Deployment template
grep -r 'marketplace.visualstudio.com\|open-vsx.org' platform/charts/code-server/templates/ returns no matches — no hardcoded external extension sources
helm lint platform/charts/code-server/ exits 0
stories.testPlan: helm lint platform/charts/code-server/ && helm template platform/charts/code-server/ | grep 'install-extension' && grep -A5 'extensions:' platform/charts/code-server/values.yaml && grep 'extensionRegistry' platform/charts/code-server/values.yaml && grep -r 'marketplace.visualstudio.com' platform/charts/code-server/templates/ && echo 'PASS: no external extension sources'

stories.title: SMART guidance: ACs asserting vendor API field values must cite chart version CRD spec
stories.description: TEST-004b failed review three times in sprint 37 because AC3 asserted 'phase=Finished' — the documented chaos-mesh phase — but v2.6.3 uses 'Not Injected' as the terminal recovery state. The implementation was correct; the AC was wrong. Root cause: no SMART gate requires that ACs asserting specific API field values (phase names, condition strings, status keys) be cross-referenced against the pinned chart version's CRD spec or empirically verified before the story is written. Add an explicit rule to scripts/ralph/ceremonies/smart.md: when an AC asserts a vendor-specific status field value (e.g. 'phase=X', 'condition=Y'), the story must either cite the upstream CRD documentation for the pinned chart version or note that the value was empirically confirmed against a running instance.
acceptanceCriteria: grep -n 'CRD\|vendor.*field\|phase.*value\|status.*field' scripts/ralph/ceremonies/smart.md returns at least one match confirming the guidance is present
grep -n 'chart version\|pinned version\|empirically' scripts/ralph/ceremonies/smart.md returns at least one match
python3 -m py_compile scripts/ralph/ceremonies.py exits 0
stories.testPlan: grep -n 'CRD\|vendor.*field' scripts/ralph/ceremonies/smart.md; grep -n 'chart version\|empirically' scripts/ralph/ceremonies/smart.md; python3 -m py_compile scripts/ralph/ceremonies.py; show all output verbatim. All three must return matches or exit 0.
stories.returnedReason: TEST-004b failed review 3x because AC3 asserted a phase name not present in the actual chart version; SMART guidance does not require CRD-spec citation for vendor API field assertions

smart.notes: All SMART dimensions green. ACs are exact grep commands against a specific file. No cluster required.

stories.title: Fix attempts field — initialize to 0, increment only on review re-open
stories.description: The attempts field is currently initialized to 1 after first implementation, causing advance.py to report 0% first-pass rate for all stories. Fix: initialize to 0 in story schema, increment only when review ceremony re-opens a story (adds a reviewNotes entry). Update the formula in advance.py to use len(reviewNotes) == 0 as the first-pass check.
acceptanceCriteria: prd/schema/story.schema.json has attempts with default 0
scripts/ralph/lib/advance.py uses len(story.get('reviewNotes', [])) == 0 for first-pass rate calculation
python3 -c "import json; s=json.load(open('prd/schema/story.schema.json')); assert s['properties']['attempts']['default'] == 0" exits 0
shellcheck or python3 -m py_compile scripts/ralph/lib/advance.py exits 0
stories.testPlan: Show schema default and advance.py formula change. Run py_compile. Show output verbatim.
stories.returnedReason: Implemented (passes: true) but review ceremony did not run before retro fired. Review ceremony in next sprint can accept it immediately.

stories.title: Chart-iterating shell scripts must be validated against all existing platform/charts before passes:true
stories.description: HA-001 failed review twice because scripts/ha-gate.sh used set -euo pipefail with a bare grep pipeline. When processing platform/charts/_globals/ (which has no top-level replicaCount field), grep exited 1, and pipefail killed the script silently. The test plan only covered a synthetic fixture chart — it never ran against the actual chart corpus. This story adds an explicit requirement to the SMART/AC guidelines: any shell script story that iterates platform/charts/ must include 'run against all existing charts in platform/charts/' as an acceptance criterion (not just synthetic fixtures). Update the relevant ceremony docs or story schema notes to enforce this.
acceptanceCriteria: scripts/ralph/ceremonies/smart.md (or equivalent SMART guidance doc) contains text requiring that chart-iterating scripts be tested against all existing platform/charts/ before marking passes:true
The requirement text includes an explicit note about bash set -euo pipefail with grep pipelines: use '|| true' when grep results may be empty
grep -n 'platform/charts' scripts/ralph/ceremonies/smart.md (or equivalent) returns at least one match confirming the guidance is present
stories.testPlan: grep -n 'platform/charts' scripts/ralph/ceremonies/smart.md and grep -n 'pipefail\||| true' scripts/ralph/ceremonies/smart.md — show output verbatim. Both must return matches.
stories.returnedReason: Root cause: shell script stories iterating the chart corpus only required synthetic fixture tests, missing existing charts with no replicaCount field

stories.title: code-server chart: PersistentVolumeClaim for /home/coder so workspace survives pod restarts
stories.description: Add platform/charts/code-server/templates/pvc.yaml: a PersistentVolumeClaim for the code-server workspace. The storageClass must reference {{ .Values.global.storageClass }} (never hardcoded). Size must be configurable via values.yaml (default 5Gi). Update the Deployment template to add a volumeMount at /home/coder and a corresponding volume referencing the PVC. This ensures that agent workspaces, cloned git repos, and shell history survive pod restarts.
acceptanceCriteria: platform/charts/code-server/templates/pvc.yaml exists — ls platform/charts/code-server/templates/pvc.yaml exits 0
helm template platform/charts/code-server/ shows a PersistentVolumeClaim with storageClassName: {{ .Values.global.storageClass }}
helm template platform/charts/code-server/ shows a VolumeMount with mountPath: /home/coder
platform/charts/code-server/values.yaml has a persistence.size field — grep 'persistence:' platform/charts/code-server/values.yaml exits 0
helm lint platform/charts/code-server/ exits 0
stories.testPlan: helm lint platform/charts/code-server/ && helm template platform/charts/code-server/ | grep -A3 'PersistentVolumeClaim' && helm template platform/charts/code-server/ | grep '/home/coder' && grep 'persistence:' platform/charts/code-server/values.yaml; show all output verbatim

stories.title: Chaos Mesh PodChaos experiment YAML + README validates PDB prevents simultaneous pod eviction
stories.description: Create test/chaos/pdb-validation.yaml: a Chaos Mesh PodChaos experiment targeting a platform service (e.g. grafana namespace, mode: all) to simultaneously terminate all pods. The PDB minAvailable:1 should prevent the last pod from being evicted. Create test/chaos/README.md explaining how to apply the experiment against a kind cluster running Chaos Mesh (TEST-004), what PDB-enforcement behavior to observe, and how to clean up. The YAML is a static test artifact — it does not run in CI. This covers the 'PDB chaos validation' goal from E15.
acceptanceCriteria: test/chaos/pdb-validation.yaml exists — ls test/chaos/pdb-validation.yaml exits 0
python3 -c "import yaml, sys; yaml.safe_load(open(sys.argv[1]).read())" test/chaos/pdb-validation.yaml exits 0 (valid YAML)
grep 'kind: PodChaos' test/chaos/pdb-validation.yaml exits 0
grep 'mode: all' test/chaos/pdb-validation.yaml exits 0 (targets all replicas to exercise PDB)
test/chaos/README.md exists and grep -i 'PodDisruptionBudget\|PDB' test/chaos/README.md exits 0
stories.testPlan: ls test/chaos/pdb-validation.yaml test/chaos/README.md && python3 -c "import yaml, sys; yaml.safe_load(open(sys.argv[1]).read())" test/chaos/pdb-validation.yaml && grep 'kind: PodChaos' test/chaos/pdb-validation.yaml && grep 'mode: all' test/chaos/pdb-validation.yaml && grep -i 'PDB' test/chaos/README.md; show all output verbatim

stories.title: cluster/kind/bootstrap.sh: kind cluster provisioner with --help, --cluster-name, and kind create cluster invocation
stories.description: Create cluster/kind/bootstrap.sh. The script must: (1) support --help flag that prints usage including '--cluster-name NAME (default: sovereign-test)'; (2) accept --cluster-name NAME option defaulting to 'sovereign-test'; (3) print 'Bootstrapping kind cluster: $cluster_name' before running; (4) invoke 'kind create cluster --name "$cluster_name"'. Script must pass shellcheck -S error with set -euo pipefail at the top. Also verify that bootstrap/providers/ is not present in the working tree (it should have been removed in RESTRUCTURE-001a).
acceptanceCriteria: cluster/kind/bootstrap.sh exists: ls cluster/kind/bootstrap.sh exits 0
shellcheck -S error cluster/kind/bootstrap.sh exits 0
bash -n cluster/kind/bootstrap.sh exits 0
bash cluster/kind/bootstrap.sh --help 2>&1 | grep -q 'cluster-name' exits 0
grep -q 'kind create cluster' cluster/kind/bootstrap.sh exits 0 (script contains the kind invocation)
[ ! -d bootstrap/providers ] exits 0 (bootstrap/providers/ not in working tree)
stories.testPlan: ls cluster/kind/bootstrap.sh && shellcheck -S error cluster/kind/bootstrap.sh && bash -n cluster/kind/bootstrap.sh && bash cluster/kind/bootstrap.sh --help 2>&1 | grep 'cluster-name' && grep 'kind create cluster' cluster/kind/bootstrap.sh && [ ! -d bootstrap/providers ] && echo 'all checks passed'; show all output verbatim

smart.notes: Specific score 4: exact file named, --help behavior and kind invocation explicitly required. Measurable score 4: shellcheck, bash -n, and grep verify defined behavior.

stories.title: platform/deploy.sh: Helm deployment orchestrator with --help, --chart-dir, --namespace, and helm upgrade --install invocation
stories.description: Create platform/deploy.sh. The script must: (1) support --help flag that prints usage including '--chart-dir DIR' and '--namespace NS'; (2) accept --chart-dir DIR and --namespace NS required options; (3) print 'Deploying chart: $chart_dir to namespace: $namespace' before running; (4) invoke 'helm upgrade --install' for the given chart and namespace. Script must pass shellcheck -S error with set -euo pipefail at the top.
acceptanceCriteria: platform/deploy.sh exists: ls platform/deploy.sh exits 0
shellcheck -S error platform/deploy.sh exits 0
bash -n platform/deploy.sh exits 0
bash platform/deploy.sh --help 2>&1 | grep -q 'chart-dir' exits 0
grep -q 'helm upgrade --install' platform/deploy.sh exits 0 (script contains the helm invocation)
stories.testPlan: ls platform/deploy.sh && shellcheck -S error platform/deploy.sh && bash -n platform/deploy.sh && bash platform/deploy.sh --help 2>&1 | grep 'chart-dir' && grep 'helm upgrade --install' platform/deploy.sh && echo 'all checks passed'; show all output verbatim

smart.notes: Specific score 4: exact file named, --help behavior and helm invocation explicitly required. Measurable score 4: shellcheck, bash -n, and grep verify defined behavior.
